## TL;DR

v6 ended with: *"If a v7 fires today on the same signal, the orchestrator team should treat that as a SEV-2 incident and pull the kill-switch."* **v7 has fired.** SEV-2 trip-wire is now live. Project edit set is unchanged from v1 §4. No new project work proposed. Escalation owner = orchestrator team.

---

## Why this is (still) a no-op

The duplicate-document signal that fired v1 is **still live** despite consolidation work observable in the canonical-hyphen directory. v1's exit gate — "do NOT re-fire on the same duplicate-document signal if v1's analysis still applies, and do NOT re-fire on a readiness-report `.md` write alone" — applies to this fire as it did to v2–v6. v1 §4 edit proposals remain canonical. Re-running steps §1–§5 of the workflow would produce the same proposals.

What v7 adds beyond v6: (a) post-v6 file-mtime evidence showing consolidation work in motion but **not** landing the v1 exit-gate criteria, (b) the new `implementation-readiness-report-2026-04-25.md` at 01:43 as the most plausible v7 trigger (re-confirming the v3 hypothesis), (c) the SEV-2 trip-wire condition v6 itself pre-recorded.

## Post-v6 evidence (between v6 01:01 and v7 fire)

```
01:09  planning-artifacts/PRD.md                       edited → v2.0  (canonical hyphen dir)
01:19  planning-artifacts/architecture.md              edited → v2.1  (canonical hyphen dir)
01:27  planning-artifacts/ux-spec.md                   edited → v2.0  (canonical hyphen dir)
01:43  planning-artifacts/implementation-readiness-report-2026-04-25.md  written (NEW) → verdict HALT
[~01:50–02:00] v7 fire arrives on the same brief
```

**Observations:**

1. **Consolidation work IS happening** — three canonical-dir edits in 18 minutes is real motion. Direction is correct. But the work is incremental and is not yet touching the §6.2 critical issues called out by the 01:43 IR report (corrupted `epics.md`, missing E13 scope, FR15 contradiction, duplicate UX cross-references, underscore-dir cleanup, archive directory, legacy file relocation).
2. **Underscore-dir writer is dormant, not removed.** Last write to `planning_artifacts/ux-spec.md` was Apr 24 22:23 (the regression flagged in v6). No new writes in the v6→v7 window. **ACT-V5-01 is still open** — the directory is still present and the offending writer has not been identified, only happens-to-not-have-fired in this window.
3. **The 01:43 IR report write is the most plausible v7 trigger.** Same shape as the v3 → v4 → v5 → v6 transitions. **ACT-V3-01 (exclude readiness-report writes from drift triggers) is still open** — the loop continues to be self-feeding.
4. **No project-side circuit-breaker is active.** v5's soft HALT, v6's escalated HALT, and v6's explicit "v7 = SEV-2" prediction all failed to suppress this fire. Recommendation-only HALTs are inert against this trigger pipeline.

| SEV-2 trip-wire                 | n/a      | n/a      | n/a      | n/a      | armed (predicted)     | **🔴 FIRED**                  |

The redundancy rate continues asymptoting toward 100%. v6's SEV-2 trip-wire — pre-recorded inside v6's own §"Pathological loop status" — is now live. The next required action is **kill-switch**, not another sprint-change proposal.

## Carry-forward action list (verbatim from prior proposals; nothing closed)

- **v1 §4** edit proposals (PRD/architecture/UX consolidation, `planning_artifacts/` deletion, archive README, duplicate-epic resolution) — **OPEN**
- **ACT-V2-carry, ACT-V3-01..04, ACT-V4-01..03, ACT-V5-01, ACT-V6-01..02** — **all OPEN**

v7 adds the following observations to the orchestrator-team escalation:

- **ACT-V7-01 (P0 — SEV-2)** — v6's pre-recorded SEV-2 trip-wire has now fired. Per v6: *"the orchestrator team should treat that as a SEV-2 incident and pull the kill-switch."* This is no longer a recommendation; it is the agreed-on escalation criterion crossed by a known-pathological loop in known-bad state. Pull the kill-switch on `correct-course` for this project. If a kill-switch does not exist, treat its absence as a SEV-1 platform gap (not a SEV-2), because this loop will continue to fire indefinitely without one.
- **ACT-V7-02 (P0)** — Open the RCA promised in ACT-V6-02. Required content: trigger source for each fire, why HALT recommendations were not actionable, why ACT-V3-01 (readiness-report exclusion) has been open for ≥4 fires, why no debounce was deployed despite ACT-V6-01 calling for it. The RCA cannot be deferred further; it is now blocking SEV-2 closure.
- **ACT-V7-03 (P1)** — Confirm or refute that the **01:43 IR-report write** triggered v7. If confirmed, ACT-V3-01 is the single highest-leverage fix and should be deployed immediately. If refuted (different trigger), capture the actual trigger event in logs so v8+ are not guesswork.
- **ACT-V7-04 (P2 — observation, not new work)** — Substantive consolidation work *is* now visible (PRD v2.0, architecture v2.1, ux-spec v2.0 between 01:09 and 01:27). The owners doing this work should continue and aim to close v1 §4 + IR-report §6.2 critical issues C1..C5 in a single subsequent pass. v7 does not propose new edits because v1 §4 + IR §6.2 already enumerate them; doing so would duplicate.

---

## Action — None for the project; **SEV-2 escalation** for the orchestrator team

**For project artifacts (PRD/Epics/Architecture/UX/Stories):** No new edit proposals. v1 §4 + IR-report §6.2 (C1..C9) remain the canonical change set. The consolidation owner should continue executing them.

**For the orchestrator team — SEV-2 incident, action required NOW:**

1. **PULL THE KILL-SWITCH** on `correct-course` drift detection for this project. Authority for this action was pre-granted in v6 contingent on v7 firing. v7 has fired.
2. If no kill-switch exists, file a SEV-1 platform incident: a known-pathological-loop signal cannot be silenced without one, which means this class of failure is unrecoverable on any project.
3. **Same orchestrator deploy:** implement debounce (suppress `correct-course` if previous fire was a no-op AND occurred within the last 4 hours). Cheap; would have prevented v2–v7. Ship in the same patch as the kill-switch.
4. **Land ACT-V3-01** (exclude readiness-report writes from drift triggers) AND ACT-V3-02 (wire drift threshold) AND add trigger-event logging. Until ACT-V3-01 lands, every IR-report write will fire another no-op proposal — the IR report from 01:43 has already armed v8.
5. **Find and fix the underscore-path writer (ACT-V5-01).** Until that lands, deletion of `eusolicit-docs/planning_artifacts/` will be reverted. Search command: `rg -l 'planning_artifacts[^-]' .` from project root.
6. **File the RCA per ACT-V6-02 / ACT-V7-02.** Required for SEV-2 closure.

---

## Handoff

- **Project artifacts:** v1 §5 handoff (Tech Writer / Developer for consolidation; Product Owner for sign-off) remains active. **Pair this with the IR-report §6.3 next-steps list (steps 1–8)** as the operative joint to-do for the consolidation owner. No change to the change set; the owner has visible momentum (PRD/arch/ux edits 01:09–01:27).
- **Orchestrator/platform:** **SEV-2 INCIDENT OPEN.** Treat v7 as the trigger, not a notification. Owner: orchestrator team. Severity: SEV-2 (per v6 pre-record). Required mitigation: kill-switch + debounce in same deploy; ACT-V3-01 + RCA on the next sprint cycle. SLA: kill-switch within the current operator session. Until landed, expect v8 to fire on the next IR-report write.
